trivia_bullets.text: Valgavoth is the elder moth demon who is both the master of and the physical substance of Duskmourn itself — his wings merge with the walls, his hunger for fear drives the House's architecture. The flavor text names him explicitly: 'All who enter the House hear Valgavoth's whispers, worming through their consciousness until they can no longer resist his influence.' Duskmourn's Domination is the mechanical expression of that mind-invasion: you permanently control the enchanted creature and it loses all abilities. [Scryfall flavor_text; Wizards.com Planeswalker's Guide to Duskmourn via WebSearch; mtg.wiki/page/Valgavoth via WebSearch]
sources: WebSearch: Valgavoth lore summary (Wizards.com Planeswalker's Guide, mtg.wiki)

trivia_bullets.text: Flavor text: "All who enter the House hear Valgavoth's whispers, worming through their consciousness until they can no longer resist his influence." — [Scryfall]. Valgavoth is described in the Planeswalker's Guide as able to tailor his horrors to each individual's deepest fears, making his domination deeply personal rather than generic. The card's -3/-0 debuff and ability-stripping mechanically represents a mind broken by sustained psychic siege. [Scryfall flavor_text; WebSearch: Valgavoth lore]

Duskmourn's Domination is a 6-mana ({4}{U}{U}) Aura that grants permanent control of the enchanted creature with -3/-0 and loss of all abilities. At 6 mana it sits in the expensive range for control-steal Auras (compare Control Magic at {3}{U}, Volition Reins at {3}{U}{U}). EDHREC rank 22,580 reflects it as a rarely-played Commander option — the high cost and vulnerability to enchantment removal make it a flavor inclusion rather than a competitive pick. [Scryfall mana_cost, cmc, oracle_text, edhrec_rank]

The card was previewed on September 10, 2024 by community figure @katiekarlov on Twitter, one week before the full DSK set release. Artist Eli Minaya's expressionistic painterly style — heavy brushwork, abstract indigo-violet palette, calligraphic script halos — differs markedly from the photorealistic-creature approach common elsewhere in DSK, making this one of the more visually distinctive commons/uncommons in the set. [Scryfall preview field; Scryfall artist field]
